title: 'Abigail' Surpasses Universal's Last Two Vampire Films at the Box Office
text: Universal's vampire film Abigail has surpassed the studio's previous two vampire releases at the box office. The movie earned $28.5 million worldwide so far, officially surpassing the global hauls of last year's Renfield and The Last Voyage of the Demeter, which earned $26.4 million and $21.7 million, respectively. Despite this milestone, Abigail faces financial hurdles. With a $28 million budget, the film needs stronger performance to break even. However, critical reception has been favorable—it achieved an 83% critical score on Rotten Tomatoes—and audience interest remains solid with only dropping 49.5% on its second weekend. The article notes that Universal's previous vampire projects struggled due to poor audience reception. The studio may shift away from the vampire genre, focusing instead on other classic monster properties like the forthcoming Wolf Man remake.
